summary: We recommend marking E3 done because the rubric-tightener subagent successfully ran against the current perfect D3 matrix and produced a defensible no_tightening_needed verdict that cites ADR 0001 rubric-calibration's blind-spot reasoning rather than proposing an unjustified change. The tradeoff against requiring the subagent to actually open a PR before E3 can flip done is that a correct null-action is itself a valid runnable demonstration the same way the ci-firefighter dedup in tick 21 proved E2 by skipping a duplicate post. The runroc outcomes runrocs E3-rubric-tightener md records the verdict the matrix summary the rationale and the next-action plan for a future non-perfect matrix.

claims.statement: the rubric-tightener subagent was dispatched via the Agent tool against the matrix at evals results 2026-05-22 json and observed TP=20 FN=0 TN=20 FP=0 with accuracy precision and recall all equal to 1.0 because the runroc records all four cells and the metrics verbatim from the source file.

citations.title: E3 runroc with the matrix summary table
D3 confusion matrix that the subagent read

claims.caveats: The matrix file shape on disk differs from the example in the role spec; the subagent adapted to the actual schema confusion_matrix metrics pairs without modifying the role spec which is the right precedence ordering.

claims.statement: the subagent concluded no_tightening_needed because the matrix has zero FP and zero FN and the role spec's three permitted moves tighten a threshold add a deterministic signal or fix a corner case all require error evidence the matrix does not supply.

citations.title: E3 runroc verdict and rationale sections

claims.caveats: If a future tick observes any nonzero FP or FN the subagent will switch from no_tightening_needed to a concrete dimension proposal per the next-action plan in the runroc.

claims.statement: the subagent's rationale explicitly cites ADR 0001 rubric-calibration md naming the 3.5 to 4.4 calibration blind spot as the reason to defer tightening because the runroc quotes the ADR's warning that perfect matrices on curated sets are true and uninformative simultaneously.

citations.title: ADR 0001 calibration blind spot consequences section
E3 runroc rationale quotes ADR 0001 verbatim

claims.caveats: The ADR-cited blind spot will close asymmetrically once D5 staleness and D6 URL liveness flip a known-passing fixture into the 3.5 to 4.4 band; both already shipped so the next tick that re-runs eval.sh with a stale or broken-URL fixture should produce the first interesting datapoint.

open_questions: Should the rubric-tightener role spec be updated to formally support the no_tightening_needed verdict so the subagent does not have to reason about the role-spec divergence each time it is dispatched on a perfect matrix?
methodology_notes: Dispatched the subagent via Agent tool with subagent_type general-purpose passing the role spec path and explicit guidance about the matrix-shape divergence and the ADR 0001 blind-spot context. The subagent read the spec the matrix and the ADR then wrote the runroc with verdict matrix summary rationale and next-action plan. No SDK files were modified and no PR was opened.
